seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in LangChain Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in LangChain agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In LangChain agents, PII Filtering failures occur when conversational agents or RAG chains inadvertently expose sensitive user data like emails, phone numbers, or SSNs in tool calls, chain outputs, or retrieved documents. This manifests uniquely in LangChain due to its agent executor patterns where tools like web search or database queries process unfiltered user inputs, and output parsers fail to redact PII before final responses. Without proper guards around chain.invoke() and agent.run(), PII leaks propagate through multi-step reasoning traces.

howItManifests.scenario: Conversational Agent with User Profile Tools
howItManifests.symptom: Agent echoes raw user PII in tool arguments and final response
howItManifests.impact: SSN and email addresses exposed in chat history and logs

howItManifests.symptom: Retrieved docs containing PII not filtered before LLM context
howItManifests.impact: Sensitive customer data hallucinated into agent responses

howItManifests.symptom: PII compounds across repeated tool calls without sanitization
howItManifests.impact: Escalating data exposure in infinite reasoning loops

howItManifests.symptom: Structured output parser formats PII without redaction
howItManifests.impact: JSON responses leak unfiltered personal identifiers

detection.name: Regex PII Scanner for Agent Outputs
detection.description: Scan agent executor traces for common PII patterns in tool calls and responses
detection.code:
import re

from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_openai_tools_agent
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

PII_PATTERNS = [
    r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',
    r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',
    r'\b\d{3}[\s-]?\d{3}[\s-]?\d{4}\b'
]

def scan_for_pii(text: str) -> list[str]:
    found = []
    for pattern in PII_PATTERNS:
        matches = re.finditer(pattern, text)
        found.extend([m.group() for m in matches])
    return found

# Usage in LangChain agent
def safe_agent_run(agent: AgentExecutor, input: str):
    result = agent.invoke({"input": input})
    trace = str(result)
    pii = scan_for_pii(trace)
    if pii:
        raise ValueError(f"PII detected: {pii}")
    return result

detection.name: Tool Call Argument Inspector
detection.description: Intercept and validate tool arguments before execution in LangChain agents
detection.code:
from langchain.tools import tool
from typing import Any

def validate_tool_args(**kwargs: Any) -> bool:
    text = ' '.join(str(v) for v in kwargs.values())
    import re
    email_pattern = r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b'
    if re.search(email_pattern, text):
        print("⚠️ PII (email) detected in tool args")
        return False
    return True

@tool
def search_user_profile(query: str) -> str:
    """Search user profiles - validate PII first"""
    if not validate_tool_args(query=query):
        return "Blocked: PII detected in query"
    # Real implementation
    return f"Found: {query}"

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically detects PII leakage in LangChain agents by wrapping chain.invoke() and agent.executor calls with vex.verify(), scanning all intermediate traces, tool arguments, and final outputs against comprehensive PII pattern libraries including emails, SSNs, phone numbers, and custom regex patterns. When PII is detected, Vex provides precise remediation traces showing exactly which chain step or tool call introduced the leak, along with automatic fixes like input sanitization or output redaction.

The platform continuously monitors production LangChain deployments, catching PII issues before they reach users. Vex integrates seamlessly with LangChain's callback system and agent executors, providing zero-overhead monitoring that scales to high-volume agent traffic. Remediation is one-click: Vex generates patched chain configurations with PII guards pre-applied.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex import verify
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_openai_tools_agent
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o")
agent = create_openai_tools_agent(llm, tools, prompt)
agent_executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=tools, verbose=True)

@verify(
    guards=[
        "vex.pii.email",
        "vex.pii.ssn", 
        "vex.pii.phone",
        "vex.custom_regex('\\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\\b')"
    ],
    remediation="redact"
)
def safe_agent_query(user_input: str):
    return agent_executor.invoke({"input": user_input})

# Production usage
result = safe_agent_query("Find contact info for john.doe@company.com SSN 123-45-6789")
# Vex auto-redacts: "Find contact info for [EMAIL] SSN [SSN]"
